skill.name: frontend-api-client-with-jwt
skill.description: Guidance for designing a centralized Next.js API client that handles JWT authorization, refresh flows, and API errors.
skill.summary: Build a consistent JWT-aware API client pattern for Next.js applications.
skill.author: Claude

security_audit.summary: The static findings are documentation terms in SKILL.md, not executable code, command execution, scanning, or exfiltration behavior. One semantic concern remains: the skill lists localStorage as a JWT storage option without enough warning about XSS exposure, so publication should include a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Security-Sensitive Token Storage Guidance
medium_findings.description: Static verdict: TRUE POSITIVE as a guidance risk, not as executable malware. The skill lists browser storage options for JWT tokens, including localStorage, which can expose bearer tokens to XSS if used without strong safeguards.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly names token storage mechanisms in JWT guidance. The file is prose rather than code, so the risk is insecure implementation advice rather than direct credential access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JWT and HTTP Status Terminology
low_findings.description: Static verdict: FALSE POSITIVE. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections point to a JWT description and an HTTP 200-299 status range, with no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or hashing implementation present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain documentation text. I found no code path, crypto function, or recommendation to use a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in API Guidance
low_findings.description: Static verdict: FALSE POSITIVE. The system and network reconnaissance detections are ordinary API-client documentation about valid tokens, HTTP 401 handling, context access, error messages, refresh performance, and token tests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain no shell commands, port scanning, host discovery, probing loops, or data collection behavior. They are conceptual guidance for API request handling and tests.

content.user_title: Build JWT API Clients for Next.js
content.value_statement: Frontend teams need one reliable way to attach JWTs, refresh expired tokens, and parse API errors. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design a centralized Next.js client pattern.
actual_capabilities: Defines when a Next.js project should use a centralized JWT API client.
Outlines authorization header attachment for client-side and server-side requests.
Describes token expiration detection, refresh attempts, and refresh failure behavior.
Explains consistent parsing for authentication, authorization, and general API errors.
Covers integration patterns for React components, hooks, and shared API access.
Lists testing concerns for token attachment, refresh flows, and concurrent requests.
limitations: Provides conceptual implementation guidance, not a complete drop-in library.
Does not include framework-specific source files or runnable tests.
Requires the user to choose secure token storage for their threat model.
Assumes JWT-based authentication and is not suited for unrelated auth systems.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Calls
use_cases.description: Create a shared client pattern for headers, request options, and response parsing in a Next.js application.

use_cases.title: Plan Auth Refresh Behavior
use_cases.description: Define how expired JWTs should refresh, fail, and redirect users when sessions cannot continue.

use_cases.title: Review Frontend Auth Design
use_cases.description: Check whether an existing API layer handles token storage, errors, and concurrent refresh scenarios consistently.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Next.js project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a centralized Next.js API client for JWT authentication. Include request headers, error handling, and basic integration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Token Refresh Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design token expiration checks and refresh behavior for my Next.js API client. Include concurrent request handling and refresh failure behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Storage and Error Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my JWT token storage and API error handling plan. Flag XSS exposure, sensitive error leaks, and weak redirect behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing API Layer
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an implemented frontend client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate my existing Next.js API client design. Compare it against token attachment, refresh coordination, error parsing, cleanup, and testing expectations.

output_examples.input: Design a JWT API client for a Next.js dashboard.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering client responsibilities, authorization headers, refresh flow decisions, error categories, and integration points.

output_examples.input: Review whether my frontend should store JWTs in localStorage.
output_examples.output: A security-focused recommendation that weighs XSS exposure, cookie options, refresh token handling, and application constraints.

output_examples.input: Define tests for token refresh behavior.
output_examples.output: A concise test plan for valid tokens, expired tokens, refresh failures, concurrent requests, redirects, and cleanup.

best_practices: Prefer secure, HttpOnly cookie-based storage when the application architecture supports it.
Centralize authorization headers, refresh behavior, and error parsing in one client layer.
Test concurrent refresh requests so only one refresh attempt updates shared auth state.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter JWT attachment logic across individual components.
Do not expose raw backend error details directly to users.
Do not treat localStorage as safe token storage without an XSS threat review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete API client?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and review criteria for building a client in your project.

faq.question: Can it work with both App Router and Pages Router?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance mentions both contexts, but the implementation details depend on your project structure.

faq.question: Does it support authentication methods other than JWT?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for JWT-based authentication flows.

faq.question: Does it recommend localStorage for JWTs?
faq.answer: It lists localStorage as a possible storage mechanism, but users should prefer safer storage when possible.

faq.question: Can it help with refresh token failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers graceful refresh failure handling and redirecting users to login when needed.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning about token storage guidance and the need for secure implementation choices.
